data.title: L3Harris closes $1B Department of War convertible preferred investment in Missile Solutions
data.description:
L3Harris closed a $1.0 billion convertible preferred investment from the Department of War into its Missile Solutions business to fund expansion and R&D.

Key Highlights:

  • DoW purchased $1.0B of AJRD Series A convertible preferred stock and warrants to fund Missile Solutions growth.
  • Proceeds will expand/modernize facilities, accelerate R&D and boost production capacity for critical missile technologies.
  • Preferred shares convert to common at investor option; automatic IPO conversion at 80% of IPO price.
  • Warrants will convert to warrants exercisable for 3% of IPO company on a fully diluted basis at avg 110% of IPO price.
  • L3Harris will remain majority owner (>80%) of the MSL business and will continue to consolidate its results.

Original SEC Filing: L3HARRIS TECHNOLOGIES, INC. /DE/ [ LHX ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Honeywell reports Q1 sales $9.14B, adjusted EPS $2.45; backlog ~$38.3B, Aerospace spin-off set for June 29
data.description:
Honeywell reported first-quarter 2026 results with net sales of $9.143 billion and adjusted diluted earnings per share of $2.45. Reported EPS from continuing operations was $1.29, operating income was $1.474 billion and segment profit was $2.129 billion; backlog rose to about $38.3 billion. The company reaffirmed full-year 2026 guidance and announced the sale of its Warehouse and Workflow Solutions business and a planned Aerospace spin-off for June 29, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue (Net sales): $9,143 million for Q1 2026 (up 2% reported vs. Q1 2025).
  • Operating income: $1,474 million for Q1 2026; operating margin 16.1% (down 320 bps vs. prior year).
  • Segment profit: $2,129 million for Q1 2026; segment margin 23.3% (up 90 bps vs. prior year).
  • Net income attributable to company: $821 million; Earnings per share — continuing operations (diluted): $1.29.
  • Adjusted diluted EPS (non-GAAP): $2.45 for Q1 2026 (up 11% vs. Q1 2025).

Business Highlights

  • Orders grew 7% organically in Q1, driving backlog to approximately $38.3 billion, with strength in Building and Industrial Automation.
  • Aerospace Technologies: sales $4,322 million (4% reported growth; 3% organic); segment profit expanded with a 26.5% margin and book-to-bill of 1.1x.
  • Building Automation: sales $1,882 million (11% reported; 8% organic) with margin expansion driven by services and fire business growth.
  • Process Automation and Technology: sales $1,513 million (5% reported; down 6% organic) with mix effects from LNG growth and regional project timing in the Middle East.
  • Industrial Automation: sales $1,421 million (reported decline reflecting divestiture impacts; 1% organic growth) with margin improvement from productivity and commercial actions.
  • Portfolio actions: announced sale of Warehouse and Workflow Solutions to American Industrial Partners and previously announced sale of Productivity Solutions and Services; Honeywell Aerospace separation planned for June 29, 2026.

Original SEC Filing: HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC [ HON ] - 8-K - Apr. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Community Health Systems launches up to $600M tender offer for 2031 and 2032 senior secured notes
data.description:
Community Health Systems announced a cash tender offer to repurchase up to $600,000,000 of its senior secured notes.

Key Highlights:

  • Wholly owned subsidiary commenced a tender offer to buy up to $600,000,000 aggregate purchase price of outstanding notes.
  • Offer caps: up to $350M for 4.750% Senior Secured Notes due 2031 and up to $250M for 10.875% Senior Secured Notes due 2032.
  • Total consideration includes Early Tender Payment of $50 per $1,000; early total = $950 for 2031 and $1,082.50 for 2032 per $1,000.
  • Tender financed with cash on hand; Early Tender Date is May 5, 2026 and Expiration Date is May 20, 2026 (expected settlement May 7 and May 22).

Original SEC Filing: COMMUNITY HEALTH SYSTEMS INC [ CYH ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Dow posts smaller first-quarter loss on polyethylene strength
data.description:
Dow Inc posted a smaller-than-expected first-quarter adjusted loss on Thursday, as the chemicals maker benefited from higher polyethylene volumes and cost-cutting measures.

The company has also been reevaluating its ownership of non-product-producing assets across its global portfolio, including power and steam production and pipelines, as the chemical industry struggles with higher feedstock and energy costs, as well as weak demand in key end markets.

"We are already seeing rapid positive momentum from our announced pricing actions in every business and every region, as well as constructive impacts to our operating rates," said CEO Jim Fitterling.

The Michigan-based company reported an adjusted loss of 14 cents per share for the quarter ended March 31, compared with analysts' average estimate of a loss of 29 cents per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: French Private Sector Contracts Most In 14 Months
data.description:
PARIS (dpa-AFX) - France's private sector shrank the most since early 2025 in April reflecting deterioration in the service sector as manufacturing output grew at the strongest pace in over four years, data from S&P Global showed Thursday.

The flash composite output index fell to a 14-month low of 47.6 in April from 48.8 in March. The score was forecast to fall to 48.6.

The services Purchasing Managers' Index dropped more-than-expected to 46.5 in April from 48.8 a month ago. The reading was seen at 48.5.

The manufacturing PMI posted a 47-month high of 52.8 in April, up from 50.0 in March. The score was above forecast of 49.5.

'The service economy has deteriorated due to a diminishing willingness to spend - a typical consequence of uncertainty - pulling overall business activity levels lower,' S&P Global Market Intelligence Principal Economist Joe Hayes said.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise as Oil Prices Move Higher — Market Talk
data.description:
1013 GMT - U.S. Treasury yields and the dollar rise alongside oil prices. The standoff in the Middle East, including the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, keeps inflation fears alive. "Tensions remain elevated in the [Middle East] region amid continued incidents in the Strait of Hormuz and a failed attempt for a second round of talks this week, undermining prospects for a near-term resolution," The Revacy Fund's Zaheer Anwari says in a note. "Overall uncertainty could continue to drive demand for the dollar as a safe-haven asset," he says. The two-year Treasury yield is up 2.3 basis points at 3.816%, while the 10-year yield rises 2.7bps to 4.320%, according to Tradeweb. The DXY dollar index is up 0.1% at 98.704. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

1000 GMT - TD Securities buys 10-year German Bunds at a yield of 3.02%, expecting the yield to fall on a one-month horizon, senior European and U.K. rates strategist Pooja Kumra says in a note. TD Securities targets a fall to 2.75%, placing a stop loss at 3.15%, she says. "We still see 3% as a cap for 10-year Bund yields," she says. The premia placed on German Bunds in 2025 in anticipation of a German growth pickup have started to unwind, Kumra says. Meanwhile, interest-rate hikes by the European Central Bank are "rather well priced," she says. The 10-year Bund yield rises 2.7 basis points to 3.023%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0937 GMT - The Philippine central bank has signaled further rate hikes to come, ANZ Research's Sanjay Mathur and Kausani Basak say in a note. Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas Gov. Eli Remolona says higher fuel prices have started to spill over into other sectors, and inflation expectations have begun rising. "Following today's rate hike, the BSP faces a challenging task in preventing the de-anchoring of inflation expectations while continuing to support growth," ANZ says. The central bank also says further policy moves will be guided by data and it will likely hike again, as external challenges remain unabated.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0918 GMT - Renewed caution surrounding the Iran war has triggered a more defensive tone across risky assets and encouraged investors to take profits in bitcoin after recent gains, LMAX Group's Joel Kruger says in a note. However, bitcoin's ability to hold above $76,000 is a key technical development, he says. "From a flow and positioning perspective, there are increasing signs that longer-term holders are stepping back in, helping to absorb supply and stabilize the market on dips." The shift follows an extended period of weak sentiment and deleveraging, which appears to have reset positioning and created a healthier base for accumulation, he says. Bitcoin falls 1.1% to $77,591 after reaching an 11-week high of $79,480 Wednesday, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0901 GMT - Sterling turns higher against the euro while gilt yields rise after the U.K. composite purchasing managers' index unexpectedly rose further into expansionary territory in April. The key measure of manufacturing and services activity rose to a two-month high of 52.0 in April from 50.3 in March. Economists in a WSJ survey had expected a reading of 49.9. "The improved rate of expansion is in part a reflection of a short-term boost from a rush to secure purchases ahead of feared price rises and supply shortages linked to the war," S&P economist Chris Williamson says in the survey. The euro falls 0.1% to 0.8663 pounds from 0.8674 before the data. The 10-year gilt yield rises to a fresh 3.5-week high of 4.952%, from 4.914% before the data, Tradeweb data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0857 GMT - Singapore's consumer inflation could overshoot 2% in 2Q and 3Q, Maybank economists say in a note. Headline inflation rose to 1.8% on year in March, due to higher private transport costs. Inflation will likely rise further and broaden to more products in the coming months, as higher energy and raw material costs from the Gulf's supply disruptions pass through supply chains. "Firms in the non-transport and utilities sectors will eventually pass on the higher input costs to consumers," they say. Maybank maintains its 2026 core and headline inflation forecasts for Singapore at 1.9% and 1.8%, respectively, around the official 1.5%-2.5% range's midpoint. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0846 GMT - The Philippine central bank is unlikely to tighten its monetary policy further if the war in the Middle East ends soon, economists at Capital Economics say in a note. The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas hiked its rates as concerns about the inflation outlook outweighed policymakers' fears about weak growth. Policymakers are likely concerned about falling behind the curve in tackling inflation pressures, although global energy prices have fallen and the peso has stabilized since the BSP's off-cycle meeting in March. Concerns about the inflation outlook should ease if the war ends and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz normalizes soon. This would prompt the BSP to shift their attention back to bolstering growth.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0835 GMT - U.K. government borrowing in the fiscal year 2026 was slightly below the forecast by the Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR), a positive development for Treasury Chief Rachel Reeves, Pantheon Macroeconomics' Elliot Jordan-Doak says in a note. However, the lower public borrowing is likely to be short-lived due to the effects of the Middle East conflict, he says. Rising inflation and expectations of interest-rate rises by the Bank of England are likely to drive up public borrowing costs. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0823 GMT - The euro and European government bonds are little moved even after the latest eurozone purchasing managers' index survey came in weaker than expected. The eurozone composite PMI fell to a 17-month low 48.6 in April from 50.7 in March, indicating that private sector activity swung into contraction. Economists in a WSJ survey expected a reading of 50.0. Weaker French and German composite PMI prints for April were released earlier. The euro trades steady at $1.1702. The 10-year Bund yield is at 3.020%, up 2.3 basis points versus 3.017% beforehand. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0819 GMT - The rebound in South Korea's 1Q GDP growth to 1.7% indicates that trade momentum remains strong, Goldman Sachs economists say. They raise their 2026 growth forecast to 2.5% from 1.9%, noting that the optimism reflects a significant improvement in the terms of trade, supported by gains in semiconductor prices. Contribution from net exports rose to 1.1 percentage points, the highest since 2Q 2023, they note, pointing to a tech-driven rebound in outbound shipments. Gains in equipment investment were driven by semiconductors and display, as well as corporate purchases of electric vehicles, they add. While a detailed breakdown isn't released in the advanced estimate, the economists point to central bank commentary noting that semiconductors accounted for around half of South Korea's manufacturing gains. (jihye.lee@wsj.com)

0814 GMT - Public sector net borrowing fell in March, indicating that U.K.'s rigid fiscal rules have been having the desired effect, Quilter's Lindsay James says in a note. Data show that public sector net borrowing declined to 12.6 billion pounds ($17 billion) in March from 14 billion pounds in March 2025. "Borrowing had been expected to be lower this year as the government had front-loaded a lot of its spending plans into its early years," James says. However the Middle East conflict could lead to a deterioration in U.K. public finances by driving up borrowing costs, she says. Ten-year gilt yields rise 2 basis points to last at 4.910%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0748 GMT - The euro falls modestly after a key measure of German manufacturing and services activity swung into contraction in April. The composite purchasing managers' index fell to a 16-month low of 48.3 in April from 51.9 in March. A level below 50 signals a contraction in activity while a level above that indicates growth. Earlier data showed a fall in the French composite PMI to 47.6 in April from 48.8 in March. The euro falls 0.1% to $1.1694 after the German data, from $1.1701 beforehand. The 10-year German Bund yield is at 3.018%, up 2.1 basis points on the day, having traded at 3.025% beforehand, according to Tradeweb. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: U.K. PMIs Suggest Inflation Could Peak Around 5% — Market Talk
data.description: The U.K.'s flash PMI surveys for April point to higher-than-expected cost pressures for businesses, says Paul Dales at Capital Economics in a note. "That is roughly consistent with CPI inflation rising to a peak of around 5.0%," he says. This is higher than Capital Economics' baseline forecast for a peak of 4.0%. The data suggest the economy is holding up stronger than anticipated, with the composite PMI rebounding to 52.0 in April from 50.3 in March, Dales says. This could lead some Bank of England policymakers to worry more about the risks of higher inflation than the risks of weaker growth, he says. Still, strong PMIs could reflect pre-war momentum and stockpiling, meaning activity could still weaken ahead, he notes. (don.forbes@wsj.com)

data.title: American Airlines Reports Earnings Thursday. It's Tough Out There. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Callum Keown

American Airlines reports earnings Thursday against an increasingly difficult backdrop for U.S. carriers amid surging jet fuel prices.

Airlines initially appeared resilient as operators navigated the turbulence brought on by the Iran war. However, earnings season and prolonged disruption to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz is starting to hit home for investors.

Major U.S. carriers have all either suspended full-year guidance, cut it, or--in Southwest's case--declined to update it.

American is up next before the open Thursday. The only saving grace for investors may be that the stock is already down 25% so far this year--underperforming Delta Air Lines' 1.4% drop and United Airlines 18% fall.

American guided for a full-year adjusted loss of between $1.70 and $2.70 per share back in January. That forecast is under serious threat now.

Analysts are expecting an adjusted loss of 47 cents per share on revenue of $13.8 billion in the first quarter, according to a FactSet poll. For the second quarter, Wall Street is looking for a loss of 8 cents per share on revenue of $16.4 billion.

The company may also face questions around a potential merger following a report that United CEO Scott Kirby pitched the idea of a tie-up between the two to senior government officials in February. American rejected the idea last week.

Delta Air Lines got earnings season off to a good start earlier this month--though the stock was helped by the U.S., Iran cease-fire being announced just hours before it reported results. But it's been downhill ever since.

United Airlines stock slumped 5.6% Wednesday after the carrier lowered its full-year guidance, while Southwest was also falling in premarket trading Thursday as it said fuel prices needed to fall and revenue to rise to meet its original outlook. Alaska Air also withdrew its guidance this week.

The U.S. Global Jets exchange-traded fund has fallen more than 7% this week, and is down 7.8% in 2026.

American will need to pull out a big positive surprise to shift the mood around the airline sector.

Write to Callum Keown at callum.keown@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Netflix announces $25 billion share buyback
data.description:
Netflix on Thursday said its board authorized an additional $25 billion share repurchase program, on top of a buyback approved in December 2024, with no expiration date.

Shares of the streaming giant rose 1.5% in premarket trading.

Netflix had previously said it planned to resume share repurchases while investing about $20 billion this year in films and television, after walking away from a deal to buy Warner Bros Discovery assets.

data.title: Canada's Mullen Group beats Q1 adjusted profit expectations on logistics & warehousing strength
data.description:
Overview

  • Canada logistics provider's Q1 revenue rose 10%, slightly missing analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS and adjusted net income for Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Revenue growth driven by acquisitions, while organic revenue from existing units declined

Outlook

  • Company says demand is steady and supply is tightening in trucking and logistics sectors

  • Mullen Group sees potential for improved pricing if current market trends continue

  • Company expects acquisitions to continue driving growth until major infrastructure projects begin

Result Drivers

  • ACQUISITIONS - Co said acquisitions, especially Cole Group, were the main driver of revenue and operating income growth in Q1

  • SEGMENT PERFORMANCE VARIATION - Revenue and profit gains in Logistics & Warehousing and U.S. & International Logistics segments were offset by declines in Less-Than-Truckload and Specialized & Industrial Services, due to demarketing, weather, and lack of repeat projects

  • COST PRESSURES AND WEATHER IMPACT - Co cited increased cost pressures and challenging winter road conditions as factors affecting results in some segments

data.title: Russian-controlled court jails woman for buying Ukrainian war bonds
data.description:
A woman living in a Russian-controlled part of southern Ukraine has been found guilty of treason and sentenced to 14 years in prison for buying war bonds to support the Ukrainian military, the court that convicted her said on Thursday.

The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia District Court said the woman had used a Ukrainian mobile app to purchase bonds worth 270,080 roubles ($3,600), and had been caught by the FSB security service.

It identified her only by the initial B, and said she was a Russian citizen. People living in parts of Ukraine that Russian forces have captured in more than four years of war have effectively been forced to take Russian citizenship if they want to retain access to healthcare, pensions and property rights.

Human rights group Memorial, which Russia this month branded an extremist movement, named the woman as 66-year-old Larisa Belyayeva, a doctor from the village of Lyubimovka.

Ukraine began issuing war bonds in February 2022, immediately after Russia's full-scale invasion, in order to fund its defence.

They have been promoted as a patriotic investment with the potential for attractive tax-free yields - currently up to 17.45% for bonds denominated in Ukrainian hryvnia, and more than 4% for dollar-denominated paper.

($1 = 74.9000 roubles)

data.title: CEZ plans partial sale of non-production assets, report says
data.description:
Czech power utility CEZ is preparing to spin off non-production assets such as electricity, gas distribution and trading, and sell up to 49% of the unit to investors, news website irozhlas.cz reported on Thursday.

CEZ and Industry Minister Karel Havlicek declined to comment on the report, which cited unidentified sources as saying the move could raise funds for a share buyback that would give the state full control over the company's power generation assets.

CEZ shares were up 1.3% at 0926 GMT.

The government owns about 70% of CEZ, which has a market capitalisation of around $31 billion. The current government has said it wants full control of the firm, or at least its electricity generation business.

Havlicek told reporters on the sidelines of a conference on Thursday that this remained the government's goal within its term running until 2029. The government has said any buyout of minority shareholders would be funded from CEZ's own resources.

Prime Minister Andrej Babis said initial steps would be taken at CEZ's annual general meeting, which the company called for June 1 on Wednesday.

Irozhlas.cz said the spinoff plan was due to be discussed at the meeting.

The new unit, which would be led by CEZ Deputy Chairman Pavel Cyrani, could be floated in an initial public offering or offered directly to investors such as international infrastructure funds, the report said.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 3:30 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 1.09% to 77,664 and the broader NSE Nifty 50 index declined 0.84% to 24,173.05, as Brent crude topped $100 a barrel after Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz and peace talks showed no sign of resuming.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee dropped 0.3% versus the U.S. dollar to 94.1050, falling for the fourth consecutive session on Thursday, its longest losing streak since mid-January, as stalled peace talks between the U.S. and Iran and oil prices over $100 per barrel weighed on sentiment.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 96.755 rupees, with the yield up 3 bps at 6.9535%.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up nearly 7 basis points at 5.8925%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose nearly 8 bps to 6.4925%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 4.65% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.13%

data.title: Rupee bogged down in worst losing streak in 3 months as oil pain persists
data.description:
By Jaspreet Kalra

The Indian rupee fell for the fourth consecutive session on Thursday, its longest losing streak since mid-January, as stalled peace talks between the U.S. and Iran lifted oil prices over $100 per barrel.

The rupee closed at 94.1050 against the U.S. dollar, down 0.3% on the day. The currency has declined over 1% so far this the week, pressured by a near 15% rise in crude prices.

Elevated energy costs also weighed down stocks, with India's benchmark Nifty 50 declining 0.8% on Thursday, tracking a similar decline in MSCI's gauge of regional equities (.MIAP00000PUS).

Iran has captured two container ships seeking to exit the Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, tightening its grip on the crucial waterway, as investors assess whether a fragile ceasefire with the U.S. will hold.

"The reassuring element is that at least one party - the U.S. - is signalling a strong desire to resume negotiations swiftly. What is less reassuring is the lack of clarity around plans for reopening the Strait of Hormuz," ING said in a note.

The rupee has also been bogged down by a partial rollback of supportive measures, which traders said has sent mixed signals to market participants, despite lingering geopolitical worries.

Meanwhile, business survey data released on Thursday showed that growth in India's private sector accelerated in April as manufacturing and services activity rebounded after cooling last month.

"Our sense is that it is a case of front-loading production," Pranjul Bhandari, chief India economist at HSBC, said in a note. "Consumers may want to purchase before retail prices are raised significantly, leading to a rise in new orders."

The firm reckons that if oil prices average about $80 per barrel in the fiscal year ending March 2027, the Reserve Bank of India is unlikely to hike rates, but a rise in average prices above $100 may put monetary tightening in play.

data.title: FirstCash Q1 revenue beats analyst estimates on pawn segment growth
data.description:
Overview

  • US pawn store operator's Q1 revenue rose 26%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 increased 30% and beat analyst expectations

  • Company declared $0.42/share quarterly dividend and repurchased $50 mln in stock

Outlook

  • FirstCash raises 2026 pawn fee revenue guidance to mid-teen growth for U.S. segment

  • Company expects Latin America pawn fees to grow at a high-teen rate in 2026

  • FirstCash increases 2026 U.K. pawn segment income guidance to $125 mln-$135 mln

Result Drivers

  • PAWN SEGMENT GROWTH - Strong performances in U.S., Latin America, and U.K. pawn segments drove record revenue and earnings, with same-store pawn receivables up 19% in the U.S., 30% in Latin America, and 29% in the U.K.

  • LATIN AMERICA TRENDS - Latin America segment growth was supported by inflationary pressures, reverse-migration, reduced remittances from the U.S., and favorable currency trends, resulting in a 62% increase in segment income in U.S. dollars.

  • ACQUISITIONS AND EXPANSION - Recent acquisitions, including the U.K.'s H&T, and ongoing store openings contributed to revenue and earnings growth, with 340 locations added over the past twelve months.

data.title: Resolutions at the Annual General Meeting in Bufab AB
data.description:
At Bufab’s Annual General Meeting (AGM) which was held today on 23 April 2026, the following principal resolutions were passed.

Adoption of the income statements and balance sheets and resolutions on appropriation of the company's profit and discharge from liabilityThe income statements and balance sheets for the company and the group were adopted, together with the Board of Directors` proposal for appropriation of the company's profit. The dividend was determined to SEK 1.30 per share, with 27 April 2026 as the record date. The estimated date for payment of the dividend is 30 April 2026. The Board of Directors and the CEO were discharged from liability for the financial year 2025.

Board of Directors, remuneration to the Board members and auditorIn accordance with the Nomination Committee's proposal, the AGM resolved that the Board of Directors shall consist of seven members elected by the meeting and no deputies. The AGM further resolved, in accordance with the Nomination Committee’s proposal, that the fees to the Board members shall amount to SEK 1,050,000 to the Chairman of the Board, and SEK 450,000 to each of the other members not employed by the company. For committee work, it was resolved that a remuneration of SEK 165,000 shall be paid to the Chairman of the Audit Committee, that a remuneration of SEK 72,000 shall be paid to each of the other members of the Audit Committee, that a remuneration of SEK 52,000 shall be paid to the Chairman of the Remuneration Committee and that a remuneration of SEK 36,000 shall be paid to each of the other members of the Remuneration Committee.

In accordance with the Nomination Committee’s proposal, the AGM resolved on re-election of the Board members Bengt Liljedahl, Hans Björstrand, Anna Liljedahl, Eva Nilsagård, Bertil Persson and Caroline Reuterskiöld, and new election of Christer Wahlquist, all for the period until the close of the next Annual General Meeting. Bengt Liljedahl was re-elected as Chairman of the Board.

The AGM resolved in accordance with the Nomination Committee’s proposal, for the period up to and including the Annual General Meeting 2027, on re-election of Öhrlings PriceWaterhouseCoopers AB as the company’s auditor, with authorised public accountant Johan Rippe as auditor in charge, to be paid according to approved invoice.

Remuneration reportThe AGM approved the Board of Directors’ report regarding remuneration pursuant to Chapter 8, Section 53 a of the Swedish Companies Act.

Guidelines for remuneration to senior executivesIn accordance with the Board of Directors’ proposal, the AGM resolved on revised guidelines for remuneration to senior executives.

Long-term share-based incentive programmeIn accordance with the Board of Directors’ proposal, the AGM resolved to adopt a long-term share-based incentive programme by way of a performance-based share savings programme for the CEO, senior executives and certain other key employees within the group.

The programme shall comprise a maximum of 640,666 shares, corresponding to approximately 0.34 percent of the total number of outstanding shares in the company. Participation in the programme requires a personal investment by the participants in so-called investment shares, where the possible investment amount depends on which participation category the participant belongs to. The programme contains two performance targets: (1) Bufab's EBITA growth where EBITA for the financial year 2025 is compared to EBITA for the financial year 2028, and (2) Total Shareholder Return (TSR) for Bufab's share between the interim reports for the first quarter of 2026 and 2029. Depending on the fulfilment of these pre-determined performance targets, the participants will, after the expiry of a three-year vesting period, be allotted a number of performance shares in the company free of charge, provided also, inter alia, that they remain employed in the Bufab group, in accordance with the terms of the programme.

To ensure Bufab's delivery of performance shares as well as costs for social security contributions, it was resolved that the Board of Directors is authorised to, with deviation from the shareholders' preferential rights, resolve on acquisition and transfer of own shares to the participants in the company's incentive programmes.

Authorisation to transfer shares in the companyIn accordance with the Board of Directors’ proposal, the AGM resolved to authorise the Board of Directors to, on one or more occasions during the period up until the next Annual General Meeting, resolve upon transfer of the company’s own shares.

For further information, please contact:

Marcus Söderberg

CFO

+46 370 69 69 00

marcus.soderberg@bufab.com

About Bufab

Bufab is a trading company that offers its customers a full-service solution as Supply Chain Partner for sourcing, quality control, sustainability and logistics for C-Parts. Bufab was founded in 1977 in Småland, Sweden, and is an international group that today consists of more than 60 companies. The group has 1,900 employees in 31 countries and annual sales of SEK 8.1 billion in 2025. The share is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm since 2014. Read more on www.bufabgroup.com.

Attachments

Resolutions at the Annual General Meeting in Bufab AB

data.title: REPLY S.p.A.: Shareholders' Meeting Approves the 2025 Financial Statements
data.description:
• Consolidated turnover of EUR2,483.6 million (EUR2,300.5 million in 2024);
  • Group net profits of EUR250.9 million (EUR211.1 million in 2024).

Approval of the proposal to distribute a dividend of EUR1.35 per share.

Approval of the plan for the purchase and/or disposal of treasury shares.

TURIN, Italy--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 23, 2026--

The General Shareholders' meeting of Reply S.p.A. [EXM, STAR: REY] held today approved the Financial Statements for the financial year 2025, confirming the distribution of a gross dividend of EUR1.35 per share.

The dividend will be paid on 20 May 2026, with dividend date set on 18 May 2026 (record date on 19 May 2026).

Approval of the 2025 financial statements

The Reply Group closed the 2025 financial year with a consolidated turnover of EUR2,483.6 million, recording a 8.0% increase compared to EUR2,300.5 million in 2024.

Consolidated EBITDA was EUR467.6 million, up 13.9% compared to EUR410.6 million recorded for the year 2024.

EBIT, from January to December, was EUR397.1 million, up 18.5% compared to EUR330.4 million recorded for the year 2024.

The Group net profit was at EUR250.9 million. In 2024, the corresponding figure was EUR211.1 million.

The Shareholders' Meeting also approved the following resolutions, in accordance with the proposals formulated by the Board of Directors:

Authorization to purchase treasury shares and continuation of the share buyback program

The Shareholders' Meeting has authorised the purchase of treasury shares, subject to the simultaneous revocation of the previous authorisation granted by the Shareholders' Meeting of 23 April 2025, ensuring continuity with the previous resolution and with the share buyback programme launched on 13 April.

The authorisation, valid for 18 months from today, relates to a maximum of 3,607,950 ordinary shares (equal to 9.64398% of the current share capital) with a nominal value of EUR0.13 each, for a maximum nominal value of EUR469,033.50, for a maximum financial commitment of EUR550,000,000. As at the date of this press release, the Company holds 197,692 treasury shares (equivalent to 0.52843% of the share capital).

The primary objective of the programme, in continuity with the previous authorisation, is the purchase of shares to serve share-based incentive schemes, transactions aimed at acquiring shareholdings, extraordinary financing transactions and/or the conclusion of agreements with strategic partners.

The purchase price may not be lower than the nominal value (currently EUR0.13) nor higher than the official trading price recorded on the EXM Market on the day prior to the purchase, increased by 20%.

Approval of the Remuneration Report

The Shareholders' Meeting also approved Sections I and Sections II of the Remuneration Report drawn up in accordance with Article 123-ter (paragraphs 3-bis and 6) of Legislative Decree 58/1998.

The manager in charge of drafting the company's financial reports, Dr Giuseppe Veneziano, declares in accordance with Paragraph 2 of Article 154-bis of the Consolidated Finance Act, that the accounting information contained in this press release corresponds to the company's records, ledgers and accounting records.

Reply [EXM, STAR: REY, ISIN: IT0005282865] specialises in the design and implementation of solutions based on new communication channels and digital media. As a network of highly specialised companies, Reply supports major industrial groups in the telecom and media; industry and services; banking and insurance and public sectors in defining and developing business models enabled by the new paradigms of AI, cloud computing, digital media and the internet of things. Reply's services include: consulting, system integration and digital services. www.reply.com

This press release is a translation, the Italian version will prevail.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260423883100/en/

data.title: Cuentas 10-K: Revenue $0.00M, EPS $(0.53)
data.description:
Cuentas reported zero revenue for the fiscal year, down from $0.676 million a year earlier, reflecting the company’s cessation of key wholesale and digital distribution channels; diluted loss per share improved to $(0.53) from $(1.22) as net loss narrowed to $(1.571) million. The improvement in net loss was driven by non‑operating gains and reduced impairments, while operating loss remained essentially flat. Management has pursued financing and operational pivots to support near‑term continuity and future telecom initiatives.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $0.000 million, decreased from $0.676 million in the prior year.
  • Gross loss: $(0.075) million, compared to $(0.751) million in the prior year.
  • Operating loss: $(1.894) million, essentially unchanged versus $(1.993) million in the prior year.
  • Net loss: $(1.571) million, improved from $(3.309) million in the prior year.
  • Loss per share (basic and diluted): $(0.53), improved from $(1.22) in the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue shift: Revenues fell to $0 in 2025 from $0.676 million in 2024 due to cessation of key wholesale and digital distribution channels.
  • Channel and partnerships: Company is pivoting toward Cuentas Mobile, a World Mobile joint venture and an MVNO reseller relationship (Plum) to integrate telecom, data and streaming services.
  • Operational milestone: Sale of a Brooksville land stake generated proceeds used to settle major creditor claims, supporting near‑term operational continuity.
  • Product and pilot programs: WSN (WiFi6) test site installations are underway in the New York tristate area; these pilots will inform decisions on broader deployment.
  • Capital and corporate actions: Management executed multiple convertible note financings and equity issuances to facilitate leadership transitions and to fund SEC reporting requirements and MVNO initiatives.

Original SEC Filing: Cuentas Inc. [ CUEN ] - 10-K - Apr. 22, 2026
